activities_projects.title: Comprehensive Human Development Index (HDI) Research Project
activities_projects.objective: To research and critically analyze HDI components and compare India with two other countries.
activities_projects.materials: Internet access, textbook, notebook, chart paper, calculator
steps: Research the latest HDI data for India and two countries (one developed, one developing).
Compare life expectancy, education level, and GNI per capita using tables and graphs.
Write a detailed report analyzing why differences exist and suggest improvements for India.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research and analytical skills while understanding global development comparisons.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess accuracy of data, depth of analysis, graphical representation, and quality of conclusions.

activities_projects.title: Local Area Development Audit
activities_projects.objective: To evaluate development status of the local community using key indicators.
activities_projects.materials: Survey forms, pen, mobile for photos (optional), notebook
steps: Design a survey covering income, education, healthcare, sanitation, and employment.
Collect responses from at least 15 households and document observations.
Prepare a detailed report with findings, problems identified, and realistic recommendations.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect textbook concepts with real-life local development conditions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate survey design, data analysis, practicality of suggestions, and presentation.

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Development Case Study
activities_projects.objective: To analyze a real-life environmental issue related to development.
activities_projects.materials: Newspaper articles, internet resources, chart paper
steps: Select a case such as deforestation, pollution, or water scarcity.
Study causes, impact on people, and economic consequences.
Propose sustainable solutions and present findings through a presentation or report.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of balancing development with environmental protection.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess clarity of case explanation, logical solutions, and research depth.

activities_projects.title: Economic Growth vs Human Development Research
activities_projects.objective: To examine the difference between economic growth and overall human development.
steps: Collect GDP growth data and HDI rankings of three countries.
Analyze whether high GDP always means high development.
Prepare a comparative research report with examples.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically analyze the limitations of economic growth as a sole indicator.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check quality of comparison, use of data, and clarity of conclusions.

activities_projects.title: Gender Equality and Development Study
activities_projects.objective: To explore the link between gender equality and development indicators.
steps: Research female literacy, workforce participation, and health indicators.
Compare data of two states or countries.
Analyze how gender inequality affects overall development.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of gender equality in national development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate research accuracy, data interpretation, and analytical depth.

activities_projects.title: Public Facilities Mapping Project
activities_projects.objective: To map and analyze public facilities in the community.
activities_projects.materials: Graph paper, ruler, pen, local observation
steps: Create a rough map of the locality showing schools, hospitals, roads, water sources.
Identify areas lacking facilities.
Prepare a development improvement proposal for those areas.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop spatial thinking and practical understanding of public infrastructure.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess mapping accuracy, identification of gaps, and feasibility of proposals.

activities_projects.title: Per Capita Income and Inequality Analysis
activities_projects.objective: To analyze income distribution and its effect on development.
steps: Collect income data of different groups in society.
Calculate per capita income and compare with national average.
Discuss income inequality and suggest measures to reduce it.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand limitations of per capita income and income inequality issues.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check correctness of calculations, depth of discussion, and suggested measures.

activities_projects.title: Vision 2040 Development Blueprint
activities_projects.objective: To design a long-term development plan for India.
steps: Identify current challenges in education, health, employment, and environment.
Develop realistic and innovative solutions for the next 15 years.
Present the blueprint in a structured format with justification.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply creative and strategic thinking to national development planning.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate creativity, feasibility, structure, and logical reasoning.

activities_projects.title: Rural vs Urban Development Comparative Study
activities_projects.objective: To compare development indicators between rural and urban areas.
steps: Collect data on literacy, employment, healthcare access in one rural and one urban area.
Compare findings using charts or tables.
Analyze key differences and suggest bridging measures.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand regional disparities in development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess data comparison quality, clarity of charts, and depth of analysis.

activities_projects.title: Development Documentary Project
activities_projects.objective: To create a short documentary highlighting development challenges and solutions.
activities_projects.materials: Mobile phone camera, notebook, editing app (optional)
steps: Identify a development issue in the local community.
Interview at least two people and record observations.
Create a 5–7 minute documentary with conclusions and suggested solutions.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research, communication, and creative presentation skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate content accuracy, creativity, clarity of message, and suggested solutions.
